title: STANDARD FOR COOKED CURED CHOPPED MEAT CXS 98-1981 Adopted in 1981. Revised in 1991, 2014, 2015. Amended in 2019, 2022.

sections.section: STANDARD FOR COOKED CURED CHOPPED MEAT CXS 98-1981 Adopted in 1981. Revised in 1991, 2014, 2015. Amended in 2019, 2022.
sections.summary: Document title: Standard for Cooked Cured Chopped Meat (CXS 98-1981), adopted 1981, revised 1991, 2014, 2015 and amended 2019 and 2022.

Records a 2022 amendment that replaces the previous detailed labelling requirements for non‑retail containers with a reference to the General Standard for the Labelling of Non‑Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346-2021).
Scope: applies to products designated 'Chopped Meat' that have been packed in any suitable packaging material.

Defines chopped meat as prepared from meat and/or poultry (may be smoked) with at least 50% coarsely cut pieces equivalent to grinding through ≥8 mm holes and no piece over 15 mm, may contain binders, and requires heat treatment, type of cure and packaging sufficient to prevent public health hazards and keep the product wholesome during storage, transport and sale.

Defines subsidiary terms: 'edible offal' (lists permitted items and excludes specified organs and poultry skin), 'meat' (edible part of mammals slaughtered in an abattoir), 'packaged' (containers that prevent contamination under normal handling), and 'poultry meat' (edible parts of listed domesticated birds slaughtered in an abattoir).

Specifies essential and optional ingredients, composition limits and quality factors: essential ingredients are meat/poultry (excluding offal), water and curing salts (food‑grade salt and potassium/sodium nitrite); optional ingredients and binders are listed; minimum ingoing meat content is 80% with binder and 90% without, maximum fat 35% with binder and 25% without; raw materials must be suitable and final product clean, uniformly cured and sliceable.
Lists essential ingredients for chopped meat: meat or poultry meat (excluding edible offal), water, and curing ingredients consisting of food‑grade salt and potassium or sodium nitrite.

Lists optional ingredients including edible offal, fats, pork rind, poultry meat, various carbohydrate and protein binders (grains, starches, bakery products, milk powders, egg and blood products, vegetable proteins), sweeteners (sucrose, dextrose, glucose syrup, etc.), spices/seasonings and water‑soluble aromatic hydrolyzed protein.
Specifies minimum ingoing meat content for the product: 80% when a binder is used, and 90% for products without a binder and edible offal (which may include heart, tongue or head meat from mammals).

Sets essential quality requirements: raw ingredients must be suitable for human consumption and free from objectionable odours/flavours, and the final product must be clean, substantially free from staining/ container contamination, uniformly and thoroughly cured, and capable of being sliced.
Requires that ingredients used be of a quality suitable for human consumption and free from objectionable odours and flavours.
Requires the final product to be clean, substantially free from staining and container contamination, with meat and poultry uniformly and thoroughly cured and the product capable of being sliced.

Permits preservatives, humectants and colours in accordance with Tables 1 and 2 of the General Standard for Food Additives (CXS 192-1995) for food category 08.3.2 and parent categories, allows only specified Table 3 additives, requires flavourings to follow CXG 66-2008, and applies Section 4.1 of CXS 192-1995 on carry-over conditions.

Requires compliance with the Maximum Levels in the General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995) and with Codex-established maximum residue limits for pesticides and veterinary drugs.

Recommends preparation and handling in accordance with relevant Codex hygiene texts (General Principles of Food Hygiene CXC 1-1969, Code of Hygienic Practice for Meat CXC 58-2005, etc.) including Listeria guidance, and requires compliance with any microbiological criteria established per CXG 21-1997.

States that the General Standard for the Labelling of Pre-packaged Foods (CXS 1-1985) applies, requires the food name and related mandatory declarations (see 7.1) and species identification in the ingredients list (see 7.2) to avoid misleading the consumer.

Requires the label name "Chopped Meat" (or a word naming the kind(s) of meat in descending order of proportion) and mandates declaration of binders, edible offal and the species of animal when omission would mislead the consumer.
Requires the list of ingredients to indicate the species of animals from which the meat or poultry meat (or combination) is derived.

Specifies date-marking: shelf-stable products must declare the date of minimum durability by year; non-shelf-stable products packaged for consumers or catering must declare day, month and year; and non-shelf-stable products packaged for non-retail supply must include adequate storage and distribution instructions.
Advises that labelling of non-retail containers should follow the General Standard for the Labelling of Non-Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346-2021).
Requires that methods of analysis and sampling for checking compliance follow the Recommended Methods of Analysis and Sampling (CXS 234-1999) relevant to this Standard's provisions.
